text.title: Evaluation Benchmarks — Multi-Model + Continuous Production Core Rules

rule.statement: Held-out test set MUST NOT leak into prompt-engineering iterations; rotate quarterly.
rule.rationale: Memorised test sets give false uplift signals.

rule.statement: LLM-as-judge MUST score against a written rubric, not free-form 'is this good'.
rule.rationale: Rubric-anchored scores are reproducible across judge models.

rule.statement: Every benchmark run MUST cite baseline_model_id + baseline_prompt_id + baseline_date.
rule.rationale: Without versioning, drift cannot be attributed.

rule.statement: Production sampling MUST run on 1-5% of live traffic, not 100%, and never block the user response.
rule.rationale: Cost grows linearly with sample rate; user-blocking judges add latency.

rule.statement: Drift alert MUST fire when win-rate vs baseline drops > 5 pp over a 24h rolling window.
rule.rationale: Sub-day windows oscillate; >24h misses fast regressions.
